note: Synthetic, IP-clean. A fictional 'Aurora' quality-management document set (quality manual + SOPs) in the genre of regulated GxP documentation. No real company data — the method is the point, not the data. Spans are deliberately written so a naive lexical/embedding match surfaces on-topic-but-unanswering text for several abstain cases (adversarial by construction). v0.2 expands the corpus from 9 to 18 spans on new topics (complaints, CAPA, audit, data integrity, recall, environmental monitoring, cleaning, OOS, labeling) without documenting the facts the existing abstain cases rely on being absent.

spans.text: All controlled documents are version-controlled. A document is effective only after the Quality Unit approves it and assigns an effective date.
Any change to a specification, procedure, or master record requires a change-control record reviewed and approved by the Quality Unit before the change takes effect.
Executed batch records are retained for seven years from the date of product release.
A deviation from an approved procedure must be documented in the deviation system within 24 hours of discovery.
Suppliers of materials that affect product quality must be qualified before use and re-qualified on a defined interval.
Product complaints are logged in the complaint system within one business day of receipt and assessed by the Quality Unit for whether an investigation is warranted.
Each corrective and preventive action (CAPA) is assigned an owner and a due date, and its effectiveness is reviewed by the Quality Unit before the CAPA is closed.
Internal quality audits are conducted on a scheduled basis, and audit findings are recorded with assigned corrective actions.

Records must be attributable, legible, contemporaneous, original, and accurate. An original entry is never obscured or deleted; corrections are made by a dated, signed amendment that preserves the original value.
A product recall is initiated by the Quality Unit, which uses distribution records to identify the affected lots.
Analytical balances are calibrated at an interval not to exceed six months by a qualified calibration provider.
If an instrument is found out of tolerance, an investigation is opened to assess impact on results generated since the last passing calibration.
Personnel are trained on a procedure before performing it, and training is documented in the training record before the task is performed.
Controlled documents may be shared with an external auditor through a read-only share link issued by the Quality Unit.
Classified production areas are monitored for viable and non-viable particulates against established action and alert limits; results exceeding an action limit trigger an investigation.
Shared equipment is cleaned between products according to a validated cleaning procedure, and each cleaning is recorded in the equipment log.
An out-of-specification laboratory result triggers a documented investigation before the result is accepted or rejected.
Issued printed labels are reconciled against the quantity used; excess or obsolete labels are destroyed and the destruction is recorded.
